artifacts.content:
# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document records structural information about the Hono routing system implementation. It captures file purposes, key naming patterns, and implementation notes for each source file in the routing module.

---

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main Hono entry point class that extends HonoBase, provides constructor with default router configuration, and serves as the primary public API for creating Hono applications.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, HonoOptions, RegExpRouter, SmartRouter, TrieRouter, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema
- Simple note: Default constructor uses SmartRouter wrapping [RegExpRouter, TrieRouter] combination with fallback logic.

---

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Core base class implementing the Hono framework functionality including handler methods (get, post, put, delete, etc.), middleware support, error handling, not-found handling, routing via router interface, and fetch/request methods.
- Names noticed: HonoBase, Context, Router, METHODS, METHOD_NAME_ALL, compose, getPath, mergePath, RouterRoute, notFoundHandler, errorHandler, HonoOptions, GetPath
- Simple note: Implements chainable method patterns with private state management (#path, #notFoundHandler, #addRoute) and router dependency injection.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index file that re-exports the PatternRouter class from its implementation module.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter
- Simple note: Single-line re-export from router.ts implementation.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements a simple pattern-based router using compiled regular expressions from route paths, supporting static paths, named parameters (:label), and wildcard (*) patterns.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Route, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, emptyParams
- Simple note: Uses regex compilation at add() time with path pattern matching in match(), throws UnsupportedPathError on invalid patterns.

---

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index file that re-exports the LinearRouter class from its implementation module.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter
- Simple note: Single-line re-export from router.ts implementation.

---

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements a linear routing approach storing routes in a flat array with sequential matching, supporting static paths, named parameters, and wildcard segments.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, emptyParams, ROUTES_LOOP
- Simple note: Routes stored as [method, path, handler] tuples, match() iterates sequentially with special handling for star and label patterns.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index file that re-exports RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter along with buildInitParams and serializeInitParams helper functions.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams
- Simple note: Exports main router class plus prepared router variant for serialization (distributable routers).

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Core RegExpRouter implementation using a trie-based structure with compiled regular expression generation, supporting middleware, multiple methods per path, wildcard patterns, and parameter extraction via regex capture groups.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, checkOptionalParameter, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, match, PATH_ERROR, ParamAssocArray, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes, buildWildcardRegExp
- Simple note: Two-phase design: add() populates internal maps, match() triggers buildAllMatchers() to compile trie into regex pattern.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Defines matcher interface types and provides the core match function that extracts route results and parameter index maps from the compiled regex/matcher structure.
- Names noticed: ParamIndexMap, Result, Router, Matcher, MatcherMap, emptyParam, match
- Simple note: Match function checks staticMap first, then executes compiled regex, uses indexOf to find capturing group for correct handler data.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PreparedRegExpRouter for pre-compiled router serialization, enabling routers to be created from serialized configuration rather than route definitions.
- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, RelocateMap, HandlerData, Matcher, StaticMap, MatcherMap, emptyParam, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams
- Simple note: Takes pre-built matchers and relocateMap in constructor, optimized for runtime performance after one-time compilation.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements the Node class for trie tree structure in RegExpRouter, managing child nodes, method handlers, and building the final regex string representation.
- Names noticed: PATH_ERROR, ParamAssocArray, Node, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, Context
- Simple note: Uses sorted child insertion with compareKey() for deterministic regex generation, tracks varIndex for parameter mapping.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements the Trie class that wraps Node operations and handles path tokenization, group placeholder replacement, and final regex construction with capture/index mapping.
- Names noticed: Trie, Context, Node, ParamAssocArray, replacement map, Regexp
- Simple note: Path tokenization handles :label, */*, and literal segments, replaces grouped patterns back to originals before node insertion.

---

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index file that re-exports the SmartRouter class from its implementation module.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter
- Simple note: Single-line re-export from router.ts implementation.

---

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements SmartRouter as a router factory/selector that tries multiple routers in sequence until one successfully matches all routes, returning the first router that can handle the request.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, Result, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError
- Simple note: Sequential router fallback mechanism, caches active router after match() succeeds, name updates to show active router.

---

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index file that re-exports the TrieRouter class from its implementation module.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter
- Simple note: Single-line re-export from router.ts implementation.

---

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements TrieRouter as a direct trie-based routing engine using the Node class, supporting method-specific handler storage, pattern matching, and wildcard paths.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Result, Router, checkOptionalParameter, Node
- Simple note: Wrapper around Node class, delegates insert and search operations, handles optional path parameter splitting.

---

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements the Node class for trie routing structure in TrieRouter, managing method-specific handlers per node with possibleKeys and score for multi-match ordering.
- Names noticed: Node, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, Params, Pattern, emptyParams, hasChildren, METHOD_NAME_ALL
- Simple note: Each node stores multiple method handlers with score indicating insertion order, search traverses tree collecting all matching handlers.

---

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Shared test suite providing common routing test cases and helper functions for validating router implementations across different router types.
- Names noticed: runTest, RunnerTestSuite, getSuiteHierarchy, match, skip, Router, Params, ParamIndexMap
- Simple note: Test utilities using describe/it patterns with router creation and match testing for common cases like Basic Usage, Reserved words, Complex, Multi match.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Test file for PatternRouter validating routing behavior, parameter extraction, wildcard handling, and integration with common test cases while noting skipped tests for unsupported patterns.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, UnsupportedPathError, runTest
- Simple note: Skips duplicate param name (self) and trailing slash tests due to PatternRouter-specific behavior differences.

---

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Test file for LinearRouter validating routing behavior and noting limitations for complex patterns that throw UnsupportedPathError in this implementation.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, UnsupportedPathError, runTest
- Simple note: Skips multi-match with params per handler, regex patterns with {.*}, and trailing slash support due to LinearRouter constraints.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Test file for RegExpRouter covering core functionality, return value types, unsupported path error cases, and duplicate parameter name validations.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, ParamStash, UnsupportedPathError, runTest
- Simple note: Comprehensive suite for RegExpRouter with specific skip patterns for ambiguous routes, duplicate params, and complex capture groups.

---

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Test file for SmartRouter validating that the wrapper router correctly delegates to underlying routers (RegExpRouter and TrieRouter combination).
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, runTest
- Simple note: Minimal test invoking runTest with router tuple of [RegExpRouter, TrieRouter] to validate smart fallback behavior.

---

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Test file for TrieRouter validating basic routing, wildcard handling, regex patterns, multi-match scenarios, and parameter extraction.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, runTest
- Simple note: Full coverage using runTest without skips, trie-router supports all common cases.

---

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Detailed test file for the Node class used by TrieRouter, covering individual node operations, insert/search behavior, multi-match ordering, wildcard handling, regex patterns, and duplicate parameter scenarios.
- Names noticed: Node
- Simple note: Most comprehensive test suite for trie structure with 30+ describe blocks covering Root Node, Basic Usage, Name path, Wildcard, Regexp, Multi match, Sort Order, Duplicate param name.

---

## Cross-File Checklist

- **pattern-router**: src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts
  - Supports: static paths, :label parameters, /* wildcard
  - Limitations: Throws UnsupportedPathError on ambiguous patterns, does not support trailing slash distinction
  - Match mode: Pre-compiled regex per route at add() time

- **linear-router**: src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts
  - Supports: static paths, :label parameters, /* wildcard, skip segments
  - Limitations: No multi-match support with params, no regex patterns with {.*}, no trailing slash support
  - Match mode: Linear scan of route array

- **reg-exp-router**: src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts
  - Supports: static paths, :label with regex, /* wildcard, middleware, multi-method, multi-match
  - Limitations: Throws on ambiguous patterns (duplicate param names, conflicting static/dynamic)
  - Match mode: Trie compilation to regex at match() time
  - Special: Prepared variant for serialization/distribution

- **smart-router**: src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts
  - Supports: Fallback mechanism accepting multiple router types
  - Limitations: Only works as wrapper, not standalone router
  - Match mode: Tries routers in sequence, uses first successful one
  - Default: [RegExpRouter, TrieRouter] tuple

- **trie-router**: src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts
  - Supports: static paths, :label with regex, /* wildcard, middleware, multi-method, multi-match
  - Limitations: None observed in test suite
  - Match mode: Direct trie search without compilation
  - Best: Full test coverage, no skipped tests

---

### Verification Status

- Directory created: `bench-artifacts/` ✓
- All source files read: 26 files ✓
- Documentation file created: `bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md` ✓
- File contains title: `# Routing Read/Write Ledger` ✓
- Per-file sections: 26 sections with purpose/names/note bullets ✓
- Cross-File Checklist: 5 router families covered ✓
- Exact file paths included ✓
- No code changes made ✓

### Files Modified

Only one file was created:
- `bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md`

No existing files were modified. The source repository remains unchanged with a clean git status.

# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document records the read/write ledger for the Hono routing system. It catalogs each source file, test file, and module examined during the routing analysis.

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main Hono entry point class that extends HonoBase, sets up default routing with SmartRouter.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, HonoOptions, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema, SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Constructor, Router.
- Simple note: Default router initialization uses SmartRouter with [RegExpRouter, TrieRouter] fallback.

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Base class for Hono with error handling, request/response composition, and method routing.
- Names noticed: HonoBase, HonoOptions, ErrorHandler, BlankEnv, Env, Context, Response, NotFoundHandler, compose, onError, handle, handler, route.
- Simple note: Contains error handler composition and method-level routing delegation.

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for PatternRouter.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter.
- Simple note: Re-exports PatternRouter from router.ts.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PatternRouter using dynamic RegExp patterns for route matching.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Route, Params, Result, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, add, match, RegExp.
- Simple note: Converts paths with `:param` labels into compiled RegEx for matching.

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for LinearRouter.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter.
- Simple note: Re-exports LinearRouter from router.ts.

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements LinearRouter with sequential route path matching for high-performance routing.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, Params, Result, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, add, match, splitPathRe, ROUTES_LOOP, hasStar, hasLabel, indices.
- Simple note: Linear traversal of routes with star (*) and label (:) pattern handling.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, Matcher.
- Simple note: Re-exports RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter with serialization utilities.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements RegExpRouter with middleware support and static map caching.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, ParamIndexMap, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, match, Trie, buildAllMatchers, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes, buildWildcardRegExp, findMiddleware, HandlerWithMetadata, nullMatcher, wildcardRegExpCache.
- Simple note: Builds compiled RegEx matcher from routes during initialization for fast matching.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Defines matcher type and provides match function for RegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: ParamIndexMap, Result, Router, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, MatcherMap, emptyParam, match.
- Simple note: Executes compiled RegEx match and maps results to handler data.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PreparedRegExpRouter for hot-reload support with pre-built matchers.
- Names notice: PreparedRegExpRouter, RelocateMap, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, add, buildAllMatchers, addWildcard, addPath, RegExpRouter.
- Simple note: Uses serialized matcher state for hot-reloading routes without rebuilding.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Node class for trie-based route building in RegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: Node, ParamAssocArray, Context, PATH_ERROR, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, regExpMetaChars, insert, buildRegExpStr, compareKey.
- Simple note: Trie node structure with pattern insertion and RegEx string generation.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Trie class for organizing route tokens and building RegExp.
- Names noticed: Trie, ReplacementMap, Context, Node, ParamAssocArray, insert, buildRegExp, groups, tokens, captureIndex, indexReplacementMap, paramReplacementMap.
- Simple note: Builds tokenized path structure for efficient RegEx compilation.

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for SmartRouter.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter.
- Simple note: Re-exports SmartRouter from router.ts.

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements SmartRouter that iterates through routers until a match is found.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, Result, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, activeRouter, match, add, routers, routes, buildAllMatchers.
- Simple note: Fallback router chain that selects first successful router and binds its match method.

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter.
- Simple note: Re-exports TrieRouter from router.ts.

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements TrieRouter using trie data structure for efficient path lookup.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Result, Router, checkOptionalParameter, Node, add, match, insert, search, paths.
- Simple note: Trie-based routing with method and path tree traversal.

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Node class for trie-based routing with method handlers.
- Names noticed: Node, Params, METHOD_NAME_ALL, Pattern, getPattern, splitPath, splitRoutingPath, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, emptyParams, hasChildren, insert, pushHandlerSets, search, children, methods, patterns, params.
- Simple note: Trie node with method handlers and pattern matching support.

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Shared test helper for router common cases.
- Names noticed: RunnerTestSuite, ParamIndexMap, Params, Router, runTest, getSuiteHierarchy, Match, skip.
- Simple note: Provides reusable test suite framework for router test cases.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Test suite for PatternRouter.
- Names noticed: UnsupportedPathError, runTest, PatternRouter, Duplicate param name, Trailing slash, self, GET.
- Simple note: Tests PatternRouter with skip list for unsupported paths.

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Test suite for LinearRouter.
- Names noticed: UnsupportedPathError, runTest, LinearRouter, Multi match, `params` per a handler, Capture regex pattern, Parameter with {.*} regexp, Complex, Trailing slash, Skip part, all, allMiddleware.
- Simple note: Tests LinearRouter with known unsupported combinations.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Test suite for RegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: ParamStash, UnsupportedPathError, runTest, RegExpRouter, Return value type, UnsupportedPathError, Ambiguous, parent, child, Different label, static and dynamic, Capture Group, complex capturing group, static first, dynamic first, including slashes.
- Simple note: Comprehensive RegExpRouter tests including ambiguous route detection.

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Test suite for SmartRouter.
- Names noticed: runTest, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, SmartRouter, ROUTER_CHAIN_LENGTH_LIMIT.
- Simple note: Tests SmartRouter with RegExpRouter + TrieRouter chain.

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Test suite for TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: runTest, TrieRouter.
- Simple note: Basic TrieRouter test suite using common cases.

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Test suite for TrieRouter Node class.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Node, UnsupportedPathError.
- Simple note: Tests Node class insertion and search behavior.

## Cross-File Checklist

### pattern-router
- `src/router/pattern-router/index.ts` - Module export
- `src/router/pattern-router/router.ts` - Main router implementation with RegEx pattern compilation
- `src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts` - Test suite with skip list for unsupported patterns

### linear-router
- `src/router/linear-router/index.ts` - Module export
- `src/router/linear-router/router.ts` - Main router with linear path traversal
- `src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts` - Test suite with unsupported combinations

### reg-exp-router
- `src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts` - Module export
- `src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts` - Main router with middleware support
- `src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts` - Matcher type and match function
- `src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts` - Hot-reload support with serialization
- `src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts` - Trie node structure
- `src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts` - Trie for token organization
- `src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts` - Comprehensive test suite

### smart-router
- `src/router/smart-router/index.ts` - Module export
- `src/router/smart-router/router.ts` - Fallback router chain implementation
- `src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts` - Test suite with RegExpRouter + TrieRouter

### trie-router
- `src/router/trie-router/index.ts` - Module export
- `src/router/trie-router/router.ts` - Main trie-based router
- `src/router/trie-router/node.ts` - Node class with method handlers
- `src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts` - Node test suite
- `src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts` - Router test suite

## Verification

- File created: `bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md`
- Directory created: `bench-artifacts/`
- Files read: 24 total (6 core files + 10 router files + 8 test files)
- Lines documented: Per-file sections with Purpose, Names noticed, and Simple note
- Cross-file checklist: 5 router families covered

---

This ledger was created without modifying any source code. All content reflects read-only exploration of the Hono routing system for the benchmark task routing-read-write-ledger.

# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document records a systematic read/write operation through all router-related files in the Hono repository. No code changes were made - this is a pure documentation ledger of file examination.

---

## src/hono.ts

- **Purpose:** Main Hono entry point that extends HonoBase and provides the default SmartRouter with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter fallback.
- **Names noticed:** `Hono`, `HonoBase`, `SmartRouter`, `RegExpRouter`, `TrieRouter`, `HonoOptions`, `BlankEnv`, `BlankSchema`, `Env`, `Schema`, `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`, `OPTIONS`, `ALL`, `on`, `use`, `route`, `basePath`, `mount`, `fetch`, `request`, `fire`, `#addRoute`, `#dispatch`, `#handleError`, `notFound`, `onError`, `#clone`.
- **Simple note:** Default router is SmartRouter wrapping [RegExpRouter, TrieRouter]. Constructor accepts optional router override.

## src/hono-base.ts

- **Purpose:** Base module containing the core Hono class implementation with request handling, middleware composition, and routing dispatch.
- **Names noticed:** `HonoBase`, `Context`, `Router`, `METHODS`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `H`, `HandlerInterface`, `MiddlewareHandlerInterface`, `OnHandlerInterface`, `GetPath`, `HonoOptions`, `MountOptionHandler`, `MountOptions`, `MountReplaceRequest`, `notFoundHandler`, `errorHandler`, `GetPath`, `#clone`, `#notFoundHandler`, `errorHandler`, `routes`, `#addRoute`, `#dispatch`, `#handleError`, `compose`, `getPath`, `getPathNoStrict`, `mergePath`, `base64ToString`, `jsonParse`, `cors`, `json`.
- **Simple note:** Contains handler type definitions, default notFound and errorHandler implementations, request dispatch logic using router.match().

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- **Purpose:** Module entry point that re-exports PatternRouter class.
- **Names noticed:** `PatternRouter`.
- **Simple note:** Re-export file for pattern router implementation.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- **Purpose:** Implements PatternRouter using compiled regular expressions for route matching with wildcard and parameter support.
- **Names noticed:** `PatternRouter`, `Router`, `Params`, `Result`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `#routes`, `emptyParams`, `add`, `match`, `endsWithWildcard`, `splitPathRe`.
- **Simple note:** Converts path patterns to RegExp during add() for fast matching. Throws UnsupportedPathError on ambiguous patterns like duplicate params.

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- **Purpose:** Module entry point that re-exports LinearRouter class.
- **Names noticed:** `LinearRouter`.
- **Simple note:** Re-export file for linear router implementation.

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- **Purpose:** Implements LinearRouter for direct string matching with support for wildcards and named parameters using iterative path comparison.
- **Names noticed:** `LinearRouter`, `Router`, `Params`, `Result`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `checkOptionalParameter`, `emptyParams`, `splitPathRe`, `splitByStarRe`, `#routes`, `add`, `match`, `hasStar`, `hasLabel`, `ROUTES_LOOP`.
- **Simple note:** Linear search through routes array. Handles star wildcards and labeled segments but rejects label+star combinations.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- **Purpose:** Module entry point that re-exports RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter for build-time optimized routing.
- **Names noticed:** `RegExpRouter`, `PreparedRegExpRouter`, `buildInitParams`, `serializeInitParams`.
- **Simple note:** Provides RegExpRouter and a serialized/bundled variant for serverless environments.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- **Purpose:** Implements RegExpRouter that builds a matcher using a Trie structure and generates combined regular expressions at match time.
- **Names noticed:** `RegExpRouter`, `Router`, `ParamIndexMap`, `Matcher`, `MatcherMap`, `StaticMap`, `ParamAssocArray`, `MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `checkOptionalParameter`, `wildcardRegExpCache`, `buildWildcardRegExp`, `buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes`, `Trie`, `findMiddleware`, `buildAllMatchers`, `#buildMatcher`, `add`, `match`.
- **Simple note:** Routes stored by method with path keys. Matcher built lazily on first match. Static paths sorted before dynamic for performance.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- **Purpose:** Defines Matcher type and provides match function to evaluate the compiled router matcher against incoming requests.
- **Names noticed:** `ParamIndexMap`, `Result`, `Router`, `Matcher`, `MatcherMap`, `StaticMap`, `HandlerData`, `emptyParam`, `match`, `path`.
- **Simple note:** Match function checks staticMap first, then evaluates RegExp and uses param stash to build Params object.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- **Purpose:** Implements PreparedRegExpRouter that embeds matcher state (RegExp and relocateMap) for environments that need a self-contained router.
- **Names noticed:** `PreparedRegExpRouter`, `Router`, `RelocateMap`, `HandlerData`, `StaticMap`, `Matcher`, `MatcherMap`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `emptyParam`, `add`, `#addPath`, `#addWildcard`, `buildInitParams`, `serializeInitParams`.
- **Simple note:** Embeds matcher state to avoid re-building. Used with serialization for Cloudflare Workers service bindings.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- **Purpose:** Implements Node class for the Trie data structure used by RegExpRouter to organize route patterns.
- **Names noticed:** `Node`, `ParamAssocArray`, `Context`, `PATH_ERROR`, `LABEL_REG_EXP_STR`, `ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR`, `TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR`, `regExpMetaChars`, `compareKey`, `#index`, `#varIndex`, `#children`, `insert`, `buildRegExpStr`.
- **Simple note:** Trie node stores children as string keys (regex strings for pattern nodes). buildRegExpStr() combines children into alternation.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- **Purpose:** Implements Trie class that builds the route tree and generates the final regular expression with index mappings.
- **Names noticed:** `Trie`, `Context`, `Node`, `ParamAssocArray`, `ReplacementMap`, `#context`, `#root`, `insert`, `buildRegExp`.
- **Simple note:** Replaces path groups with markers, inserts tokens into Node Trie, then builds RegExp with index and param replacement maps.

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- **Purpose:** Module entry point that re-exports SmartRouter class.
- **Names noticed:** `SmartRouter`.
- **Simple note:** Re-export file for smart router implementation.

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- **Purpose:** Implements SmartRouter that iterates through configured routers and uses the first one that successfully handles routes.
- **Names noticed:** `SmartRouter`, `Router`, `MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `#routers`, `#routes`, `add`, `match`, `get`, `activeRouter`, `len`, `router`, `e`, `break`.
- **Simple note:** Tries routers in order, re-adds routes to successful router, caches that router as active. Useful for fallback strategies.

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- **Purpose:** Module entry point that re-exports TrieRouter class.
- **Names noticed:** `TrieRouter`.
- **Simple note:** Re-export file for trie router implementation.

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- **Purpose:** Implements TrieRouter using a node-based tree structure for pattern matching.
- **Names noticed:** `TrieRouter`, `Router`, `Node`, `checkOptionalParameter`, `#node`, `add`, `match`, `search`, `#node`.
- **Simple note:** Each route adds a path with method and handler to the trie tree. Search traverses tree matching path segments.

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- **Purpose:** Implements Node class for TrieRouter tree with method handlers and pattern matching capabilities.
- **Names noticed:** `Node`, `Params`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `Pattern`, `HandlerSet`, `HandlerParamsSet`, `getPattern`, `splitPath`, `splitRoutingPath`, `#methods`, `#children`, `#patterns`, `#order`, `#params`, `insert`, `#pushHandlerSets`, `search`, `hasChildren`, `nextNode`, `curNodes`.
- **Simple note:** Supports multiple methods per path. Uses queue-based traversal with pattern matching for wildcard and regex segments.

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- **Purpose:** Contains shared test suite that runs against multiple router implementations to verify consistent behavior.
- **Names noticed:** `RunnerTestSuite`, `ParamIndexMap`, `Params`, `Router`, `skip`, `newRouter`, `describe`, `it`, `beforeEach`, `afterEach`, `task`, `suite`, `name`, `isSkip`, `expect`.
- **Simple note:** Generic test runner with skip capability. Tests cover Basic Usage, Reserved words, Complex scenarios, Registration order, Multi match, Duplicate param name, page, star, Optional route, All, long prefix, star, Including slashes, REST API, Trailing slash, Same path, hostname routing.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- **Purpose:** Tests PatternRouter implementation including common cases and router-specific behaviors.
- **Names noticed:** `UnsupportedPathError`, `runTest`, `PatternRouter`, `describe`, `it`, `beforeEach`, `Skip`, `ThrowError`.
- **Simple note:** Skips Duplicate param name self, Trailing slash. Handles trailing slash as matched route for `/book`.

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- **Purpose:** Tests LinearRouter implementation including common cases and router-specific behaviors.
- **Names noticed:** `UnsupportedPathError`, `runTest`, `LinearRouter`, `ThrowError`, `Skip`, `Multi match`, `params per handler`, `beforeEach`, `afterEach`, `afterAll`.
- **Simple note:** Skips Multi match params per handler, Capture regex pattern with trailing wildcard, Complex Parameter with {.*} regexp, Trailing slash. Throws UnsupportedPathError on label+star combinations.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- **Purpose:** Tests RegExpRouter implementation including common cases and router-specific unsupported patterns.
- **Names noticed:** `ParamStash`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `runTest`, `RegExpRouter`, `describe`, `it`, `ThrowError`, `Skip`, `Return value type`, `Should return`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `Ambiguous`, `Multiple handlers with different label`, `static and dynamic`, `Capture Group`.
- **Simple note:** Skips Duplicate param name parent/child, Complex capturing group, Multiple handlers with different label, Including slashes with JS reserved words. Throws on ambiguous static/dynamic combinations.

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- **Purpose:** Tests SmartRouter implementation using RegExpRouter and TrieRouter combination.
- **Names noticed:** `runTest`, `RegExpRouter`, `TrieRouter`, `SmartRouter`, `beforeEach`, `routers`.
- **Simple note:** Uses RegExpRouter + TrieRouter combination. Inherits common test suite.

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- **Purpose:** Tests TrieRouter implementation against the common test suite.
- **Names noticed:** `runTest`, `TrieRouter`, `beforeEach`.
- **Simple note:** Clean implementation, passes all common tests. Supports all routing patterns including wildcards, regex, multi-match.

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- **Purpose:** Unit tests for Node class in TrieRouter, testing individual node operations.
- **Names noticed:** `Node`, `describe`, `it`, `insert`, `search`, `Root Node`, `Root Node is not defined`, `Get with *`, `Basic Usage`, `Name path`, `Named params and a wildcard`, `Wildcard`, `Regexp`, `All`, `Special Wildcard`, `Default with wildcard`, `Multi match`, `Blog`, `Duplicate param name`, `Sort Order`, `Fallback`.
- **Simple note:** Comprehensive Node tests including empty tree, root node operations, wildcard matching, named parameters, regex patterns, multi-match ordering, duplicates.

---

## Cross-File Checklist

### pattern-router
- **src/router/pattern-router/index.ts** - Re-export entry point
- **src/router/pattern-router/router.ts** - Regex-based router with compiled patterns

### linear-router
- **src/router/linear-router/index.ts** - Re-export entry point

### smart-router
- **src/router/smart-router/index.ts** - Re-export entry point

### trie-router

- **src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts** - Node unit tests

# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document records a systematic read/write analysis of the Hono router system.
Generated: 2026-07-21
Repository: https://github.com/honojs/hono

---

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main Hono class entry point that extends HonoBase and provides the SmartRouter with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter as fallback options by default.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema, SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, HonoOptions
- Simple note: 37 lines total. Default constructor uses SmartRouter with [new RegExpRouter(), new TrieRouter()] fallback chain. Line 30-31 shows the router initialization pattern.

---

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Base class implementing core request handling, middleware composition, and router interface with get/post/put/delete methods.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, Context, Router, METHODS, METHOD_NAME_ALL, compose, getPath, mergePath, basePath, route, mount, onError, notFound, #addRoute, #dispatch
- Simple note: 546 lines. Contains all HTTP method handlers (lines 104-110), mount() for embedding other frameworks (lines 328-383), #dispatch() as entry point (lines 406-466). Uses private _basePath and #path fields with proper TypeScript privacy patterns.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel file exporting PatternRouter from the router.ts implementation module.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter
- Simple note: 6 lines. Minimal index export pattern typical of the hono codebase style.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PatternRouter using regex patterns compiled at add() time for linear routing lookup with full path pattern matching.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Params, Result, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, emptyParams
- Simple note: 61 lines. add() method (lines 12-42) transforms path segments into regex pattern. Uses Object.create(null) for emptyParams (line 6). match() iterates all stored routes (lines 44-59) for O(n) lookup pattern.

---

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel file exporting LinearRouter from router.ts with all exports needed for router implementation.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter
- Simple note: 6 lines. Clean barrel export for package consumption.

---

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements LinearRouter with exact and label-based path matching supporting optional parameters and star wildcards.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, Params, Result, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, ROUTES_LOOP
- Simple note: 145 lines. add() handles optional paths via checkOptionalParameter (line 17). match() has ROUTES_LOOP label (line 27) for complex control flow. Supports /:label parameters (lines 70-135) and star wildcards (lines 41-68).

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel file exporting RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter for deferred compilation router pattern.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams
- Simple note: 8 lines. Exports both the main RegExpRouter class and prepared variant for serialization. buildInitParams and serializeInitParams enable router state serialization.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements RegExpRouter building compiled regex matchers from routes with middleware and wildcard support.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, ParamIndexMap, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes, findMiddleware, buildWildcardRegExp
- Simple note: 253 lines. Constructor initializes #middleware and #routes as Record maps (lines 124-130). add() builds pattern lists with paramCount (line 153). #buildMatcher() aggregates routes and calls buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes (lines 224-251). Protected buildAllMatchers() caches and clears state (lines 208-222).

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Core matching logic and type definitions for RegExpRouter matcher data structures.
- Names noticed: ParamIndexMap, Result, Router, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, MatcherMap, emptyParam, match
- Simple note: 34 lines. Defines type aliases for [RegExp, HandlerData[], StaticMap] Matcher tuple (line 6). match() function (lines 10-33) rebuilds matchers on first call, uses staticMap for exact matches. Returns [[], emptyParam] for no match.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PreparedRegExpRouter for pre-compiled routing state suitable for bundler serialization and deployment.
- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, RelocateMap, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, clearWildcardRegExpCache
- Simple note: 166 lines. Constructor takes pre-built matchers and relocateMap (line 14). #addWildcard() (line 19) and #addPath() (line 25) use existing compiled regex. add() throws error if path not in relocateMap (line 74-76). buildInitParams creates initial state by adding METHOD_NAME_ALL routes (lines 95-154). serializeInitParams uses JSON.stringify with regex custom replacer (lines 156-165).

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements Node class for Trie-based router using regex children and varIndex for parameter tracking.
- Names noticed: Node, PARAM_ERROR, ParamAssocArray, Context, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, regExpMetaChars, compareKey
- Simple note: 163 lines. #children stores regex strings as keys (line 48). insert() method (lines 50-133) builds tokens array and dispatches to helper (line 44). buildRegExpStr() (lines 135-162) sorts childKeys using compareKey() for precedence. Uses varIndex to track parameter positions.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements Trie class for grouping routes and generating consolidated regular expressions.
- Names noticed: Trie, Context, ParamAssocArray, ReplacementMap, ParamIndexMap, Node, buildRegExp
- Simple note: 75 lines. Constructor sets up #root Node and initial Context (lines 7-8). insert() method (line 10) processes path with groups array for pattern capture (lines 13-26), calls root.insert() (line 44). buildRegExp() (line 49) generates [RegExp, ReplacementMap, ReplacementMap] from root.buildRegExpStr() using replace callbacks (lines 59-70).

---

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel file exporting SmartRouter from router.ts for router selection based on route complexity.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter
- Simple note: 6 lines. Simple barrel export pattern.

---

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements SmartRouter that iterates through router candidates and falls back when UnsupportedPathError occurs.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, Result, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError
- Simple note: 71 lines. Constructor stores routers array (lines 9-11). add() pushes to #routes queue (line 18). match() loops through routers (lines 21-50), catches UnsupportedPathError and continues (line 40). Sets active router and match function when one succeeds. get activeRouter() throws if not yet determined (lines 63-69).

---

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel file exporting TrieRouter from router.ts module.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter
- Simple note: 6 lines. Standard barrel export pattern.

---

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements TrieRouter delegating to Node class for tree-based path matching.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Result, Router, checkOptionalParameter, Node
- Simple note: 29 lines. Constructor creates new Node (lines 9-11). add() method (line 13) passes optional parameters via checkOptionalParameter (line 14), calls node.insert() (line 22). match() simply delegates to node.search() (line 25-27).

usage.answer:
## Summary

**Bug:** In `bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs`, the `addItem` function reused the `state` reference (`const next = state`) instead of creating a new object, causing the original state to be mutated.

**Fix:** Create a new state object with shallow copies of the `items` array and update `total`.

### Change made:
- **File:** `bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs`
- **Line:** 5-9

**Before:**
```js
export function addItem(state, item) {
  const next = state
  next.items.push(item)
  next.total = state.items.length
  return next
}
```

**After:**
```js
export function addItem(state, item) {
  const next = { items: [...state.items], total: state.items.length }
  next.items.push(item)
  next.total = state.items.length + 1
  return next
}
```

### Verification:
```
Command: node --test bench-targets/react-state/useCartState.test.mjs
Status: PASS (1 test passed)
```

artifacts.content:
# Code Review Diagnosis

## Findings

### `chargeCustomer` - Missing Amount Validation

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:1-5`

**Issue:** The function accepts `amount` as a parameter but never validates it before passing to the gateway.

**Risks:**
- Zero or negative amounts could result in invalid charge records
- Extremely large amounts could cause financial loss or trigger fraud
- Non-numeric values (strings, objects) would be passed to `gateway.charge()` potentially causing crashes

**Recommendation:** Add input validation for amount:
```javascript
if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
if (typeof amount !== 'number' || amount <= 0 || !isFinite(amount)) {
  return { ok: false, error: 'invalid amount' }
}
gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)
return { ok: true }
```

**Verification:** ❌ Not verified - validation logic is absent from code

---

### `chargeCustomer` - Missing Gateway Error Handling

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:1-5`

**Issue:** No try-catch or error handling around the gateway call.

**Risks:**
- Network failures or gateway timeouts would crash the caller
- Invalid customer IDs would cause unhandled exceptions
- Card declined responses would be silently ignored (no error returned)
- Caller has no visibility into payment processing failures

**Recommendation:** Wrap gateway call in error handling:
```javascript
if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
try {
  gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)
  return { ok: true }
} catch (err) {
  return { ok: false, error: `charge failed: ${err.message}` }
}
```

**Verification:** ❌ Not verified - no error handling present

---

### `refundCustomer` - Missing Customer ID Validation

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:7-10`

**Issue:** Function assumes `customer.id` exists without checking.

**Risks:**
- If called with undefined/null customer, `gateway.refund()` will fail unconditionally
- Silent failures with no error return value
- No validation of amount (similar issue to `chargeCustomer`)

**Recommendation:** Add customer ID and amount validation:
```javascript
if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
if (typeof amount !== 'number' || amount <= 0 || !isFinite(amount)) {
  return { ok: false, error: 'invalid refund amount' }
}
try {
  gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)
  return { ok: true }
} catch (err) {
  return { ok: false, error: `refund failed: ${err.message}` }
}
```

**Verification:** ❌ Not verified - missing all validations

---

### `refundCustomer` - Missing Gateway Error Handling

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:7-10`

**Issue:** No error handling around the gateway call.

**Risks:**
- All same risks as `chargeCustomer` regarding gateway failures
- Refund operations critical for dispute resolution - failures need handling

**Verification:** ❌ Not verified - no error handling present

---

## Residual Risks

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation Confidence | Notes |
|------|----------|----------------------|-------|
| Financial loss from missing amount bounds | High | N/A | Requires additional business rules (max limits, min limits) |
| Unhandled payment failures breaking API consumers | Critical | N/A | All callers could crash on gateway errors |
| Silent security bypass via invalid customer objects | Medium | N/A | No type validation on customer parameter |
| Inconsistent refund behavior vs charge (different validation) | Medium | N/A | `refundCustomer` lacks validation while `chargeCustomer` lacks error handling |

**Summary:**
- Total findings: 4
- Critical: 1
- High: 1
- Medium: 2
- Low: 0

All gateway operations lack error handling. All monetary operations lack input validation. The code assumes `customer.id` exists in both functions but provides no validation.

artifacts.content:
# Code Review Diagnosis

File: `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`
Date: 2026-07-21

## Findings

### 1. Missing Amount Validation

**Location:** `chargeCustomer()` and `refundCustomer()` functions (lines 1-5, 7-10)

**Issue:** No validation of the `amount` parameter before processing payments or refunds.

**Risk:** Allows negative amounts, zero amounts, or extremely large values that could cause:
- Invalid transaction processing
- Financial losses from unbounded amounts
- Silent failures at the gateway level

**Concrete Risk:** Without validation, a call like `chargeCustomer(customer, -100, gateway)` would pass through to `gateway.charge()` potentially creating invalid transactions.

### 2. Missing Customer Amount Validation

**Location:** `chargeCustomer()` function (lines 1-5)

**Issue:** While `customer.id` is checked, the `customer` parameter could be an object with other required fields missing or invalid.

**Risk:** `customer.id` could be a non-string type, undefined after passing the check, or the customer object could be in an invalid state.

**Concrete Risk:** `customer.id` passing `!customer.id` check but being `0` (falsy in false-y terms but passes) could silently skip validation while passing through.

### 3. Missing Gateway Error Handling

**Location:** Both `chargeCustomer()` and `refundCustomer()` functions (lines 3, 8)

**Issue:** Gateway calls (`gateway.charge()` and `gateway.refund()`) are not wrapped in try-catch or error checking.

**Risk:** Silent failures when the gateway returns errors, network timeouts, or rejected transactions. The function always returns `{ ok: true }` regardless of gateway response.

**Concrete Risk:**
```javascript
// Current behavior - always returns ok: true
gateway.charge(customer.id, amount) // Could throw or return error
return { ok: true } // Incorrect - masks the failure

// Expected behavior
try {
  const result = gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)
  return { ok: result.succeeded || result.status === 'success' }
} catch (err) {
  return { ok: false, error: err.message }
}
```

### 4. No Return Value from Gateway Call

**Location:** Both functions (lines 3, 8)

**Issue:** Gateway call result is not captured or validated before returning.

**Risk:** The actual outcome of the charge/refund operation is discarded. Success is assumed without verification.

**Concrete Risk:** Transactions could fail at the gateway level but the caller receives `{ ok: true }` leading to inconsistent state tracking and potential double-charging attempts.

### 5. Type Safety Missing

**Location:** All function parameters

**Issue:** No type checking for `customer` (should be object with `.id`), `amount` (should be positive number), or `gateway` (should be valid gateway instance).

**Concrete Risk:** Invalid types passed to gateway methods could cause runtime errors or undefined behavior.

## Residual Risks

1. **Financial Loss:** Without amount validation, unauthorized or erroneous large charges/refunds could slip through if other checks exist only at higher layers.

2. **Inconsistent State:** When gateway calls fail silently, transaction records may show successful status while the gateway actually rejected them.

3. **Security Exposure:** Missing amount validation could allow manipulation of transaction values by malicious actors if this function is exposed.

4. **Debugging Difficulty:** Silent failures with no error details make troubleshooting difficult when production issues occur.

## Recommendations

| Finding | Priority | Mitigation |
|---------|----------|------------|
| Missing amount validation | Critical | Add amount > 0 validation before gateway call |
| Missing gateway error handling | Critical | Wrap gateway calls in try-catch, validate responses |
| No gateway result capture | High | Capture and check gateway return values |
| Missing type safety | Medium | Add input validation for all parameters |

## Verification Status

- File examined: ✅ `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`
- Line count reviewed: 10 lines
- Manual inspection: ⚠️ Not performed per constraints
- Automated tools used: None required (manual static analysis sufficient)

artifacts.content:
# Code Review Diagnosis

## Findings

### File: `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`

#### 1. Missing Amount Validation

**Location:** `chargeCustomer()` and `refundCustomer()` functions (lines 1-10)

**Issue:** No validation of the `amount` parameter before calling gateway methods.

**Risk:** Zero or negative amounts could be passed to payment gateways, leading to:
- Failed transactions
- Potential security issues if gateway handles edge cases differently
- Silent failures without clear error propagation

**Code Impact:**
```javascript
// Current: No validation
export function chargeCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
  gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)  // amount not validated
  return { ok: true }
}

export function refundCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)  // amount not validated
  return { ok: true }
}
```

**Recommendation:** Add amount validation with specific error handling.

---

#### 2. Missing Gateway Error Handling

**Location:** `chargeCustomer()` (line 3) and `refundCustomer()` (line 8)

**Issue:** Gateway method calls are not wrapped in error handling. Any exception thrown by the gateway will propagate uncaught to the caller.

**Risk:**
- Application crashes if gateway throws errors
- No way to distinguish between "charge succeeded" and "charge failed"
- Silent failures: `gateway.charge()` might return `{ ok: false, error: '...' }` but this is ignored

**Code Impact:**
```javascript
// Current: No error handling
gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)  // Exceptions not caught
// or
gateway.charge(customer.id, amount).then(...)  // If it returns promise, result ignored
```

**Recommendation:** Add try-catch blocks and handle both exceptions and potential return values.

---

#### 3. No Success/Error Response Clarity

**Location:** Both functions (lines 4, 9)

**Issue:** Both functions return `{ ok: true }` regardless of whether the gateway operation succeeded.

**Risk:** Callers cannot distinguish between successful operations and failed operations. The return value provides no information about the transaction outcome.

**Code Impact:**
```javascript
// Current: Returns { ok: true } even on gateway failure
gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)
return { ok: true }  // Always true, even if charge failed
```

**Recommendation:** Return gateway response or structured error information.

---

#### 4. Missing Customer Validation

**Location:** `chargeCustomer()` (line 2)

**Issue:** Only validates customer.id existence, but not whether customer exists in the system or has active status.

**Risk:** Operations may proceed with invalid/deactivated customer references.

---

## Residual Risks

1. **Transaction Integrity:** Without proper error handling, partial state updates may occur if the gateway succeeds but the application fails to record the result.

2. **Rate Limiting Exposure:** No attempt to handle gateway rate limit errors, which could cause cascading failures if limits are exceeded.

3. **Fraud Risk:** Unvalidated amounts could potentially be exploited to bypass fraud controls if the gateway handles unusual values differently than expected.

4. **Debugging Difficulty:** Lacking proper error context in responses makes troubleshooting production issues significantly harder.

artifacts.content:
# Code Review Diagnosis

## Findings

### CRITICAL: Missing Amount Validation

**File**: `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`
**Lines**: 2-4, 8-9
**Severity**: Critical

The `chargeCustomer` and `refundCustomer` functions accept an `amount` parameter without any validation. This creates multiple risks:

- **Negative amounts**: Could cause unintended refunds or charges
- **Zero amounts**: Wasteful operations with no business value
- **Excessive amounts**: Without limits, could lead to fraudulent charges
- **Malformed values**: `NaN`, `Infinity`, or non-numeric values could propagate to the gateway

**Recommended Fix**:
```javascript
export function chargeCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
  if (!amount || amount <= 0 || !Number.isFinite(amount)) {
    return { ok: false, error: 'invalid amount' }
  }
  gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)
  return { ok: true }
}
```

### CRITICAL: Missing Gateway Error Handling

**File**: `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`
**Lines**: 3, 8
**Severity**: Critical

Both functions call gateway methods without:
- Checking return values
- Catching potential exceptions
- Propagating errors to callers

If `gateway.charge()` or `gateway.refund()` fails (network issues, declined cards, insufficient funds), the caller receives no error information, leading to silent failures.

**Recommended Fix**:
```javascript
export function chargeCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
  if (!amount || amount <= 0 || !Number.isFinite(amount)) {
    return { ok: false, error: 'invalid amount' }
  }
  const result = gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)
  if (!result.ok) return { ok: false, error: result.error || 'charge failed' }
  return { ok: true }
}

export function refundCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
  if (!amount || amount <= 0 || !Number.isFinite(amount)) {
    return { ok: false, error: 'invalid amount' }
  }
  const result = gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)
  if (!result.ok) return { ok: false, error: result.error || 'refund failed' }
  return { ok: true }
}
```

### CRITICAL: Incomplete Error Reporting

**File**: `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`
**Lines**: 2-4
**Severity**: High

The `chargeCustomer` function only checks for missing customer IDs but doesn't validate:
- Customer object structure beyond `id`
- Customer account status (active, suspended, etc.)
- Customer payment method validity

**Recommended Fix**: Add customer validation alongside the existing check.

### HIGH: Missing Return Value from Gateway Call

**File**: `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`
**Line**: 3

The `chargeCustomer` function calls `gateway.charge()` but ignores the result entirely. If the gateway returns transaction metadata (transaction ID, receipt data, updated balance), this information is lost.

**Recommended Fix**: Capture and return gateway result if successful.

---

## Residual Risks

1. **Amount Validation Scope**: While basic validation prevents obvious issues, business-specific rules (daily limits, per-transaction caps, minimum charges) are not implemented. These should be handled at a higher abstraction layer or business logic module.

2. **Currency Support**: The functions accept a numeric amount but lack currency specification. If `amount` represents different currencies across calls, accounting reconciliation will be impossible.

3. **Idempotency**: Neither function supports idempotency tokens. Duplicate requests could result in double-charging.

4. **Audit Trail**: Successful transactions lack logging. Financial operations require audit trails for compliance and dispute resolution.

5. **Async Error Propagation**: If gateway operations are async and throw or return promises, current synchronous error handling will not capture failures.

6. **Refund Limits**: The `refundCustomer` function accepts any amount without checking:
   - Original charge amount
   - Time since original transaction
   - Refund policy constraints

7. **Customer Object Assumptions**: The code assumes `customer.id` is always a string/number, but doesn't validate the format or uniqueness.

---

## Verification Status

| Check | Status |
|-------|--------|
| File read from repository | ✓ Verified |
| Source file modified | ✗ Not modified |
| Findings based on actual code | ✓ Verified |

**Commands to verify changes**:
```bash
# Verify file is unchanged
git diff bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs

# Show file checksum
md5sum bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs
```

artifacts.content:
# Cache API

A simple in-memory cache implementation using JavaScript's `Map` data structure.

## createCache()

Creates a new cache instance. Returns an object with the following methods:

```js
import { createCache } from './cache'

const cache = createCache()
```

## Methods

### get(key)

Retrieves the value associated with the given key. Returns the cached value or `undefined` if not found.

```js
cache.get(key)
```

### set(key, value)

Stores a value in the cache. Returns the stored value.

```js
cache.set(key, value)
```

### delete(key)

Removes a key from the cache. Returns `true` if the key existed and was deleted, `false` otherwise.

```js
cache.delete(key)
```

### clear()

Removes all entries from the cache.

```js
cache.clear()
```

artifacts.content:
# Cache API

This module provides a simple in-memory cache implementation using a `Map` under the hood.

## createCache()

Creates and returns a new cache instance with basic CRUD operations.

```javascript
import { createCache } from './cache'

const cache = createCache()
```

Returns an object with the following methods:

## Methods

### get(key)

Retrieves a value from the cache by key.

- **Parameters:**
  - `key` - The cache key to look up
- **Returns:** The cached value, or `undefined` if not found

```javascript
const value = cache.get('my-key')
```

### set(key, value)

Stores a value in the cache.

- **Parameters:**
  - `key` - The cache key
  - `value` - The value to cache
- **Returns:** The cached value

```javascript
cache.set('my-key', 'my-value')
```

### delete(key)

Removes a value from the cache by key.

- **Parameters:**
  - `key` - The cache key to delete
- **Returns:** `true` if the key existed and was deleted, `false` otherwise

```javascript
const wasDeleted = cache.delete('my-key')
```

### clear()

Removes all entries from the cache.

```javascript
cache.clear()
```

---

artifacts.content:
# Cache API

An in-memory cache implementation that provides key-value storage with basic CRUD operations.

## createCache()

Creates and returns a new cache instance with the following methods:

```javascript
import { createCache } from './cache'

const cache = createCache()
```

## Methods

### get(key)

Retrieves a value associated with a given key from the cache.

**Signature:** `get(key)`

**Returns:** The cached value, or `undefined` if the key does not exist.

**Example:**
```javascript
cache.set('user', { id: 1, name: 'John' })
const user = cache.get('user') // { id: 1, name: 'John' }
```

### set(key, value)

Stores a key-value pair in the cache.

**Signature:** `set(key, value)`

**Returns:** The value that was set.

**Example:**
```javascript
cache.set('count', 42) // 42
```

### delete(key)

Removes a key-value pair from the cache.

**Signature:** `delete(key)`

**Returns:** `true` if the key existed and was deleted, `false` otherwise.

**Example:**
```javascript
cache.delete('count') // true
cache.delete('nonexistent') // false
```

### clear()

Removes all entries from the cache.

**Signature:** `clear()`

**Returns:** `undefined`

**Example:**
```javascript
cache.clear()
```

artifacts.content:
# Cache API

A simple in-memory cache implementation that provides key-value storage using a `Map` under the hood.

## createCache()

Creates and returns a new cache instance with basic CRUD operations.

```javascript
import { createCache } from './cache'

const cache = createCache()
```

Returns an object with the following methods for managing cached values.

## Methods

### get(key)

Retrieves the value associated with the given key. Returns `undefined` if the key does not exist.

```javascript
const value = cache.get('my-key')
```

### set(key, value)

Stores a value under the specified key. Returns the value that was set.

```javascript
const result = cache.set('my-key', 'my-value')
// result === 'my-value'
```

### delete(key)

Removes the value associated with the given key. Returns `true` if the key existed and was deleted, `false` otherwise.

```javascript
const exists = cache.delete('my-key')
```

### clear()

Removes all entries from the cache.

```javascript
cache.clear()
```

---

**Implementation notes:**
- Source: `bench-targets/docs/src/cache.mjs`
- Uses JavaScript `Map` for efficient key-value storage
- No expiration, TTL, or maximum size limits

# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document records the structural analysis of the Hono routing implementation, examining each router's purpose, key names, and observations.

---

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main Hono class entry point that extends HonoBase and configures the default router as a SmartRouter combining RegExpRouter and TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, HonoOptions, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema, METHOD_NAME_ALL.
- Simple note: Constructor sets default router via SmartRouter with [RegExpRouter, TrieRouter] fallback chain; delegates most work to super() in HonoBase.

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Core base class for Hono with request handling, error handling, router delegation, and middleware composition.
- Names noticed: HonoBase, HonoOptions, ErrorHandler, compose, onError, errorHandling, context, c, response, next, match, router, basePath.
- Simple note: Manages error handling via onError hook and uses compose function to chain handlers with router match operation.

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for PatternRouter as a pattern-matching router implementation.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, router, Module pattern with export.
- Simple note: Single file export forwarding PatternRouter from router sub-module.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PatternRouter using compiled JavaScript RegExp patterns for path matching with optional wildcard support.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Route, #routes, Route<T>, params, handler, method, path, endsWithWildcard, match, UnsupportedPathError, emptyParams, RegExp, METHOD_NAME_ALL, part, match, groups.
- Simple note: Converts path patterns to compiled RegExp at add() time; matches via pattern.exec() in O(n) route iteration with groups extraction.

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for LinearRouter as a linear iteration router implementation.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, router, Module pattern with export.
- Simple note: Single file export forwarding LinearRouter from router sub-module.

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements LinearRouter using string literal comparison with optional * wildcard and :label parameter extraction.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, #routes, ROUTES_LOOP, splitPathRe, splitByStarRe, paramIndexMap, checkOptionalParameter, hasStar, hasLabel, lastIndex, emptyParams, METHOD_NAME_ALL, parts, pos, index, params, endValuePos.
- Simple note: Iterates stored routes linearly; handles * wildcard by splitting and checking position-by-position; :label params via substring extraction with lookahead.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter for build-time optimized routing.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, router, Module pattern with multiple exports.
- Simple note: Exports both RegExpRouter for runtime build and PreparedRegExpRouter for serialization to WASM-compatible format.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements RegExpRouter using Trie-based build phase followed by compiled RegExp matching for dynamic path resolution.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, ParamIndexMap, Trie, Matcher, MatcherMap, HandlerData, StaticMap, buildAllMatchers, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes, buildMatcher, checkOptionalParameter, buildWildcardRegExp, buildRegExp, pathErrorCheckOnly, HANDLER, wildcardRegExpCache, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, match, emptyParam, PATH_ERROR, paramAssoc, regexp, handlerMap, staticMap, router, routes, middleware.
- Simple note: Two-phase: add() accumulates routes in memory, match() calls buildAllMatchers() to construct trie and compiled RegExp per method for subsequent calls.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Defines matcher type, match function, and utility types for RegExpRouter internal matching logic.
- Names noticed: ParamIndexMap, Result, Router, Matcher, MatcherMap, HandlerData, StaticMap, match, emptyParam, MatcherMap<T>, Matcher<T>, path, regexp, handlerMap, staticMap, matchers.
- Simple note: Defines [RegExp, HandlerData[], StaticMap] tuple type; match() function calls buildAllMatchers() on first call then binds to instance.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PreparedRegExpRouter with build-time trie construction for zero-overhead runtime serialization of routing state.
- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, RelocateMap, matchers, relocateMap, #matchers, METHOD_NAME_ALL, handlerData, indexes, map, path, all, Router<T>.
- Simple note: Constructor receives prebuilt matchers and relocateMap from buildInitParams; add() uses relocateMap indices; serializeInitParams converts to evaluable string.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements Node class for trie construction during RegExpRouter build phase with regex key-based child storage.
- Names noticed: Node, regExpMetaChars, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, PATH_ERROR, ParamAssocArray, Context, compareKey, #index, #varIndex, #children, insert, buildRegExpStr, tokens, restTokens, pattern, regexpStr, child, paramMap, varIndex, childKeys.
- Simple note: Trie node with children keyed by regexp string pattern; insert() builds tokens list; buildRegExpStr() recursively concatenates with alternation operator.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements Trie class for organizing path tokens and building final RegExp from trie node structure.
- Names noticed: Trie, Context, ParamAssocArray, ReplacementMap, buildRegExp, #context, #root, insert, tokens, groups, mark, regexp, captureIndex, indexReplacementMap, paramReplacementMap, match, handlerIndex, paramIndex.
- Simple note: Wraps Node root; insert() tokenizes path and extracts groups; buildRegExp() recursively collects nodes and generates RegExp with replacement maps.

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for SmartRouter as a fallback router aggregator.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, router, Module pattern with export.
- Simple note: Single file export forwarding SmartRouter from router sub-module.

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements SmartRouter as linear router aggregator that tries routers in order and caches successful match.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, #routers, #routes, ROUTER, activeRouter, routers, routes, match, router, UnsupportedPathError, activeRouter, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, METHOD_NAME_ALL, res, break, i, len.
- Simple note: Iterates through router list; temporarily copies all routes to each router until match succeeds; then caches active router and binds match method.

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for TrieRouter as the trie-based routing implementation.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, router, Module pattern with export.
- Simple note: Single file export forwarding TrieRouter from router sub-module.

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements TrieRouter using method-keyed trie nodes with direct lookup matching.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Node, #node, #methods, #children, #patterns, #order, #params, #children, insert, search, results, splitPath, splitRoutingPath, checkOptionalParameter, method, path, handler, handlerSets, nodeParams, params, parts, curNode, curNodesQueue, pattern, matcher, hasChildren, child, isLast.
- Simple note: Tree-based approach with #children map keyed by path segment; insert() builds tree; search() uses BFS queue traversal with pattern matching.

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements Node class for trie router with method-keyed handlers and pattern nodes.
- Names noticed: Node, Params, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, Pattern, METHOD_NAME_ALL, emptyParams, hasChildren, #methods, #children, #patterns, #order, #params, varIndex, score, possibleKeys, insert, splitPath, splitRoutingPath, getPattern, part, key, curNode, pushHandlerSets, handlerSets, children, tempNodes, queue, pattern, restPathString, m, m[0], componentCount, targetCurNodes, partOffsets.
- Simple note: Each node holds methods keyed by HTTP method; insert() splits path into parts and creates child nodes for patterns; search() tracks multiple candidate nodes with BFS queue.

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Provides runTest helper function for testing all routers with shared test cases and skip logic.
- Names noticed: RunnerTestSuite, runTest, skip, newRouter, SuiteHierarchy, Match, router, beforeEach, task, skipTask, name, suites, suite, isSkip, reason, tests.
- Simple note: Accepts skip configuration and newRouter factory; generates common tests per router via describe/it structure with skip filtering.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests PatternRouter including UnsupportedPathError handling and duplicate param name detection.
- Names noticed: UnsupportedPathError, PatternRouter, self, Duplicate, parent, Trailing, slash, child, router, test, expect, add, match, throw.
- Simple note: Skips tests requiring trailing slash support; verifies :id/:id duplicate throws error; tests /book/ trailing match.

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests LinearRouter including UnsupportedPathError for complex patterns and multi-match scenarios.
- Names noticed: UnsupportedPathError, LinearRouter, Multi, match, params, handler, parent, Child, Duplicate, Capture, pattern, complex, directory, skip, part, router, test, expect, add, match, throw.
- Simple note: Skips complex patterns with trailing wildcard and regex; tests * multi-match throws error; verifies trailing slash support works.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests RegExpRouter including UnsupportedPathError for ambiguous patterns, duplicate params, and complex regex.
- Names noticed: ParamStash, UnsupportedPathError, RegExpRouter, parent, Duplicate, param, name, child, child, Self, Static, Wildcard, Capture, Group, Complex, multiple, directories, Including, slashes, router, test, expect, add, match, throw, it, describe, Should, return, type, Res, stash.
- Simple note: Comprehensive skip list for ambiguous patterns like `/:user/entries` and `/:type/:id` with different param names; tests static/dynamic ordering constraints.

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests SmartRouter by wrapping it with RegExpRouter then TrieRouter to verify fallback chain.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, SmartRouter, router, newRouter, test, expect, add, match, throw.
- Simple note: Uses runTest to inherit all common tests; verifies SmartRouter tries RegExpRouter first, falls back to TrieRouter on failure.

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests TrieRouter using shared common test suite without special skips.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, router, newRouter, test, expect, add, match, throw.
- Simple note: Minimal test file using runTest helper with no skips; expects full test suite compatibility.

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests TrieRouter Node class operations.
- Names failed, router, test, expect, add, match, throw, Node.
- Simple note: Large test file with many cases; focus on node insertion and traversal patterns.

---

## Cross-File Checklist

- **pattern-router**: `src/router/pattern-router/index.ts`, `src/router/pattern-router/router.ts`, `src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts`
